title: [TASK] Set up frontend application (React, TanStack Router, TanStack Query, oRPC client, i18n)

conversation_log.summary: Task is architecturally consistent with architecture.md. Approved with three clarifications: (1) Vite is confirmed as the bundler for apps/frontend — record as ADR; (2) routeTree.gen.ts should be gitignored and generated during dev/build, not committed; (3) oRPC client packages must be compatible with @orpc/contract ^1.13.6 and zod ^4.3.6 already used in @cv-tool/contracts. No architectural changes required.

31 of 34 criteria are testable. Three criteria require revision: (7) 'renders without runtime errors when the dev server is running' — browser-observable only, no E2E tooling in scope; (12) 'Navigating to / in a browser displays...' — requires live browser/dev server, excluded by out-of-scope; (27) 'The rendered page in a browser displays the translated string value' — same browser-runtime issue. Suggested rewrites provided for all three. Routed back to Requirements.

Re-review after re-invocation. Issue body has not been updated — criteria 7, 12, and 27 remain unchanged from the original untestable wording. No revisions have been applied. Routing back to Requirements again for the required edits.

Revised 5 acceptance criteria flagged by Tester across two rejection rounds. Changes: (AC 7) Replaced 'renders without runtime errors when dev server is running' with a static check — build exit code 0 and tsc --noEmit passing, noting that runtime correctness is structurally guaranteed by criteria 5 and 31. (AC 12) Replaced 'Navigating to / in a browser displays...' with a verifiable static check — the generated route tree must include an entry for the / route confirming index route registration. (AC 27) Replaced 'rendered page in a browser displays the translated string value' with a static file inspection check — the translation key referenced by useTranslation must exist in the namespace JSON file with a non-empty string value. (AC 28) Replaced 'terminal output confirms it is listening on a port' (running-process observation) with a static check — the dev script value in package.json must include a Vite command, verifiable by reading the file. (AC 29) Replaced 'turbo run dev starts the dev server successfully' (running-process observation) with a dry-run check — turbo run build --filter=@cv-tool/frontend --dry=json must exit 0 and list the package, confirming Turborepo filter resolution. Also added 'Live browser or dev-server process verification' to the Out of Scope section to make the verification boundary explicit. All previous Architect and Tester approvals are invalidated by these changes — restarting sign-off from Architect.

decisions.approach: Scaffold apps/frontend as a React SPA with Vite, TanStack Router (file-based codegen, routeTree.gen.ts gitignored), TanStack Query, oRPC typed client against @cv-tool/contracts AppRouter, and react-i18next — all verified via static file checks, tsc --noEmit, and turbo build.
risks: routeTree.gen.ts must be generated before tsc/Vite processes it during build — build script ordering must account for this
@orpc/client version must be compatible with @orpc/contract ^1.13.6 already used in @cv-tool/contracts
VITE_ prefix required for env vars exposed to client code — VITE_API_URL must be used consistently
